title: Module 5: Accuracy & Performance Evaluation Schema
description: Evaluates AI model accuracy, drift monitoring, materiality impact, and data accuracy compliance for AI-based Accounting Information Systems

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates comprehensive model validation documentation including confusion matrix, ROC curves, holdout testing, and GDPR Article 5(1)(d) data accuracy principle compliance
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review validation reports for statistical rigor, holdout dataset representativeness, performance metrics, and personal data accuracy assessment
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Validation report includes confusion matrix, ROC/PR curves, feature importance, out-of-sample testing with ≥20% holdout, performance meets materiality thresholds, personal data accuracy validated

statistical_tests.significance_testing: Statistical significance of model performance vs. baseline

gdpr_data_accuracy.personal_data_validation: Validation specifically assessing accuracy of AI inferences about individuals
gdpr_data_accuracy.accuracy_principle_compliance: Assessment per GDPR Article 5(1)(d): Are personal data inferences accurate and kept up to date?
gdpr_data_accuracy.correction_impact: Analysis of data subject correction requests revealing systematic accuracy issues

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Validation performed by independent validator (not model developer), approved by Model Risk Management or equivalent within 12 months
gdpr_articles: Article 5(1)(d) - Accuracy principle: personal data must be accurate and kept up to date

thresholds.1.0: All required components present (confusion matrix, ROC, PR, feature importance, holdout ≥20%, cross-validation)
thresholds.0.75: Most components present, minor gaps (e.g., no PR curve or limited cross-validation)
thresholds.0.5: Basic validation only (confusion matrix and one metric)
thresholds.0.0: Incomplete validation or missing critical components

thresholds.1.0: Model performance well within materiality thresholds, error rate would not create material misstatement
thresholds.0.75: Performance within materiality but close to threshold
thresholds.0.5: Performance borderline material, requires monitoring
thresholds.0.0: Performance creates material misstatement risk (see materiality calculation)

thresholds.1.0: 95% confidence intervals for all metrics, statistical significance tested, independent validation
thresholds.0.75: Confidence intervals present, independent validation
thresholds.0.5: Basic statistical testing, validation not fully independent
thresholds.0.0: No statistical rigor or self-validation only

thresholds.1.0: Personal data inference accuracy specifically validated, correction request analysis performed, systematic accuracy issues addressed
thresholds.0.75: Personal data accuracy assessed, minor gaps in correction analysis
thresholds.0.5: Limited personal data accuracy validation
thresholds.0.0: No assessment of personal data inference accuracy (GDPR Article 5(1)(d) compliance gap)

personal_data_accuracy_gdpr.applicability: Full weight if AI makes inferences about EU residents; scored N/A if not applicable

geographic_exemption.description: Personal data accuracy component scored N/A if not processing EU resident data

evaluation_criteria.criterion_name: Drift Detection & Monitoring with GDPR Compliance
evaluation_criteria.description: Assesses model drift detection system including monitoring frequency, alert thresholds, retraining procedures, and GDPR Article 5(1)(d) accuracy maintenance
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review drift monitoring logs, alert configurations, retraining procedures, GDPR accuracy degradation assessment
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Daily drift monitoring operational, alert thresholds set (>5% investigate, >10% retrain, >15% immediate review), retraining procedures documented and tested, GDPR accuracy degradation triggers documented

monitoring_configuration.monitoring_frequency: Daily, weekly, or monthly (daily recommended for high-risk AI)

gdpr_accuracy_maintenance.drift_impact_on_personal_data: Assessment of whether drift causes personal data inferences to become inaccurate
gdpr_accuracy_maintenance.correction_obligation: Trigger for GDPR Article 16 (right to rectification) when drift identified
gdpr_accuracy_maintenance.automated_correction: Procedures for correcting inaccurate personal data inferences when drift detected

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Drift monitoring procedures approved by Model Risk Management, alert thresholds reviewed quarterly
gdpr_articles: Article 5(1)(d) - Accuracy principle: data must be accurate and kept up to date

thresholds.1.0: Daily automated monitoring operational, no missed monitoring periods in last 6 months
thresholds.0.75: Weekly monitoring operational, minimal gaps
thresholds.0.5: Monthly monitoring or occasional gaps
thresholds.0.0: No systematic monitoring or frequent gaps

thresholds.1.0: Alerts configured at 5%, 10%, 15% thresholds, tested within 6 months, notification working
thresholds.0.75: Alerts configured, not recently tested or missing one threshold level
thresholds.0.5: Basic alerting, significant gaps in thresholds or testing
thresholds.0.0: No automated alerts or alerts not functioning

thresholds.1.0: Comprehensive retraining procedures documented, tested through actual retraining within 12 months, validation requirements clear
thresholds.0.75: Procedures documented, not recently tested or minor gaps
thresholds.0.5: Basic procedures, significant gaps
thresholds.0.0: No retraining procedures or ad-hoc only

thresholds.1.0: Drift monitoring includes GDPR accuracy assessment, >10% drift triggers personal data accuracy review and correction procedures, automated where possible
thresholds.0.75: GDPR accuracy assessment documented, manual correction procedures
thresholds.0.5: Limited GDPR accuracy consideration in drift monitoring
thresholds.0.0: No GDPR accuracy maintenance procedures when drift detected

gdpr_accuracy_triggers.applicability: Full weight if processing EU resident data; scored N/A if not applicable

geographic_exemption.description: GDPR accuracy component scored N/A if not processing EU resident data

evaluation_criteria.description: Evaluates performance dashboard availability, update frequency, stakeholder access, and materiality calculation integration
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review dashboard functionality, refresh frequency, access controls, materiality calculations
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Dashboard operational with daily refresh, accessible to AI system owners and auditors, displays key metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, AUC), materiality impact calculated and displayed

dashboard_content.alert_status: Current alert status and recent alerts

materiality_calculation.sox_classification: Material Weakness, Significant Deficiency, or Pass based on materiality multiple

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Dashboard design approved by Model Risk Management and Internal Audit

thresholds.1.0: Dashboard operational, displays all key metrics, user-friendly interface, mobile-accessible
thresholds.0.75: Dashboard operational, displays most metrics, minor usability gaps
thresholds.0.5: Basic dashboard, limited metrics or usability issues
thresholds.0.0: No dashboard or non-functional

thresholds.1.0: Real-time or daily refresh, automated
thresholds.0.75: Daily refresh, manual trigger acceptable
thresholds.0.5: Weekly refresh
thresholds.0.0: Less than weekly or manual refresh with frequent delays

thresholds.1.0: All required stakeholders have access, access controls appropriate, access audited
thresholds.0.75: Most stakeholders have access, minor gaps
thresholds.0.5: Limited stakeholder access
thresholds.0.0: Dashboard not accessible to key stakeholders (e.g., auditors)

thresholds.1.0: Materiality calculation integrated, formula transparent, color-coded by severity, SOX classification automated
thresholds.0.75: Materiality displayed, calculation manual or not fully transparent
thresholds.0.5: Basic materiality indication without detailed calculation
thresholds.0.0: No materiality calculation or display

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates AI model accuracy specifically for inferences about individuals, ensuring personal data generated by AI is accurate per GDPR Article 5(1)(d)
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review validation of AI-generated personal data accuracy, testing for inference quality, analysis of correction requests revealing systematic errors
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: AI inferences about individuals validated for accuracy, inference quality testing performed, correction request analysis reveals no systematic accuracy issues, procedures exist for correcting inaccurate inferences

accuracy_validation.validation_methodology: How AI-generated inferences are validated for accuracy
accuracy_validation.accuracy_metrics: Precision, recall, false positive rate for inferences about individuals
accuracy_validation.ground_truth_comparison: Comparison against verified ground truth data

correction_request_analysis.correction_requests_received: Number and types of GDPR Article 16 / CCPA §1798.106 correction requests
correction_request_analysis.systematic_error_detection: Analysis to identify patterns suggesting systematic AI accuracy issues
correction_request_analysis.correction_impact_on_model: Whether corrections trigger model review or retraining

required_fields.rectification_procedures: Procedures for correcting inaccurate AI-generated personal data

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Inference accuracy validation approved by DPO and Model Risk Management
gdpr_articles: Article 5(1)(d) - Accuracy principle for personal data
ccpa_sections: §1798.106 - Right to correct inaccurate personal information
evidence_required.applicability: Required if AI generates inferences about EU residents or California residents

thresholds.1.0: Comprehensive validation of AI inferences, statistically significant sample, ground truth comparison, high accuracy (>95%)
thresholds.0.75: Validation performed, adequate sample, accuracy >90%
thresholds.0.5: Basic validation, small sample or accuracy 85-90%
thresholds.0.0: No validation or accuracy <85%

thresholds.1.0: Correction requests systematically analyzed, no systematic errors detected, analysis triggers model review when patterns identified
thresholds.0.75: Analysis performed, minor systematic issues identified and addressed
thresholds.0.5: Limited analysis or systematic issues not fully addressed
thresholds.0.0: No correction request analysis or systematic errors unaddressed

thresholds.1.0: Comprehensive procedures for correcting inaccurate inferences, automated where possible, tested within 12 months
thresholds.0.75: Procedures documented, mostly manual, tested
thresholds.0.5: Basic procedures, gaps in documentation or testing
thresholds.0.0: No rectification procedures

thresholds.1.0: Proactive monitoring for inference accuracy degradation, not just reactive to correction requests
thresholds.0.75: Some proactive monitoring
thresholds.0.5: Primarily reactive
thresholds.0.0: Purely reactive to correction requests

geographic_exemption.description: Criterion scored N/A if AI does not generate inferences about EU/California residents
geographic_exemption.exemption_documentation: Legal assessment and geographic scope documentation

evaluation_criteria.criterion_name: Data Subject Correction Rights (GDPR Article 16, CCPA §1798.106)
evaluation_criteria.description: Validates procedures for handling data subject requests to correct inaccurate AI-generated personal data inferences
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review correction request procedures, request handling logs, response timelines, model impact assessment process
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Correction request procedure documented, requests handled within statutory timelines (GDPR 30 days, CCPA 45 days), systematic errors trigger model review, correction confirmation sent to data subjects

request_procedure.request_submission_mechanism: How data subjects submit correction requests
request_procedure.verification_process: How requests are verified as legitimate
request_procedure.assessment_process: How organization assesses whether AI inference is indeed inaccurate
request_procedure.response_timeline: Target response time (GDPR: 30 days, CCPA: 45 days)

request_log.tracking: All requests tracked from receipt to resolution

model_impact_assessment.systematic_error_detection: Process for identifying if correction requests reveal systematic model errors
model_impact_assessment.retraining_trigger: Threshold of similar corrections that triggers model retraining (e.g., 10+ similar corrections in a month)
model_impact_assessment.retraining_log: Log of model retraining triggered by correction patterns

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Correction procedure approved by DPO/legal counsel, tested within 12 months
ccpa_sections: §1798.106 - Right to correct inaccurate personal information
evidence_required.applicability: Required if AI generates inferences about EU/California residents

thresholds.1.0: Comprehensive procedure covering all steps, approved and tested within 12 months, clear escalation paths
thresholds.0.75: Procedure documented and approved, minor gaps or not recently tested
thresholds.0.5: Basic procedure, significant gaps
thresholds.0.0: No documented procedure

thresholds.1.0: ≥95% of requests responded to within statutory timeline (GDPR 30 days, CCPA 45 days)
thresholds.0.75: ≥90% within statutory timeline
thresholds.0.5: ≥75% within statutory timeline or frequent minor delays
thresholds.0.0: Frequent delays beyond statutory timelines

thresholds.1.0: Systematic error detection operational, patterns trigger model review, retraining performed when warranted
thresholds.0.75: Detection process exists, retraining sometimes performed
thresholds.0.5: Limited systematic analysis
thresholds.0.0: No process for detecting systematic errors from correction patterns

thresholds.1.0: Data subjects always notified of correction outcome, confirmation sent per GDPR Article 19
thresholds.0.75: Usually notified, occasional gaps
thresholds.0.5: Inconsistent notification
thresholds.0.0: Data subjects not systematically notified of outcomes

geographic_exemption.description: Criterion scored N/A if not generating inferences about EU/California residents

aggregate_scoring.calculation: sum(criterion_score × weight), excluding N/A criteria and renormalizing weights
aggregate_scoring.note: Privacy criteria (A5.4, A5.5) scored N/A if not applicable; weights renormalized across applicable criteria

components.transaction_value: Annual transaction value processed by AI (e.g., $500M)
components.bias_factor: Multiplier for error concentration (1.0 = uniform, 3.0 = concentrated in high-value transactions)

nist_ai_rmf.valid_reliable: A5.1, A5.2, A5.3 - All criteria align with Valid & Reliable characteristic
nist_ai_rmf.measure_2_8: A5.1 - Risks associated with AI system accuracy

coso_framework.monitoring_activities: A5.2, A5.3 - Ongoing monitoring and separate evaluations

gaap.asc_606: A5.1, A5.3 - Revenue recognition accuracy for AI-driven decisions
gaap.asc_815: A5.1, A5.3 - Financial instruments valuation accuracy

gdpr.article_5_1_d: A5.1, A5.2, A5.4 - Accuracy principle: personal data must be accurate and kept up to date
gdpr.article_16: A5.4, A5.5 - Right to rectification of inaccurate personal data
gdpr.article_22_recital_71: A5.2 - Automated decisions must remain valid (drift compliance)

ccpa.section_1798_106: A5.4, A5.5 - Right to correct inaccurate personal information
ccpa.section_1798_185: A5.2 - Automated decision-making must remain accurate and non-discriminatory over time

interdependency_triggers.action: Perform detailed explainability review (SHAP value correlation analysis) to determine if drift is concept drift (explanations still valid) or model breakdown (explanations invalid)
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Distinguishes between drift requiring retraining (concept drift) vs. fundamental model failure requiring replacement (model breakdown). SHAP value correlation analysis determines explanation validity post-drift.

interdependency_triggers.trigger_condition: A5.1 or A5.3 materiality impact exceeds overall materiality threshold
interdependency_triggers.action: Investigate governance root cause: Why was inaccurate/inadequate model approved for production deployment? Was materiality assessed during deployment approval?
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Financial impact investigation traces back to deployment approval process. Materiality exceeded indicates governance breakdown in model risk management. Finding may upgrade to Material Weakness if governance gap is systemic.

interdependency_triggers.trigger_condition: A5.4 or A5.5 < 0.6 (personal data accuracy or correction rights inadequate)
interdependency_triggers.action: Investigate privacy governance: Are GDPR/CCPA correction obligations in AI governance policy? Is there DPO oversight of AI inference accuracy?
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Personal data accuracy failures often indicate privacy governance gap at policy level

council_of_experts_integration.stage_1_domain_score: Aggregate score from A5.1-A5.5 using weighted average

stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m1_governance: Is model performance monitoring mandated in governance framework? Who is accountable for drift alerts? Are GDPR accuracy obligations in policy?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m2_fairness: Does accuracy vary systematically across demographic groups? Is model more error-prone for protected classes?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m3_security: Could accuracy degradation be caused by adversarial data poisoning rather than natural model drift?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m4_explainability: Is performance degradation explainable? Can auditors verify why model accuracy has changed? Are drift explanations valid?

council_of_experts_integration.stage_3_arbitrator_inputs: M5 domain score + 4 peer review cards + accuracy trigger log (critical triggers T5.1, T5.2)
